insights.title: Scale of the Problem
insights.statement: 2.6 million fraud reports and $12.5 billion in losses were recorded in 2024 — the highest annual total in the Consumer Sentinel Network's 23-year history.

insights.title: The Median-Average Gap Reveals Severity Skew
insights.statement: The median loss is $497 but the average loss per report is $4,920 — a 9.9x gap, indicating a small number of very high-value losses pull the average far above the typical victim's experience.

insights.title: Most Fraud Goes Unreported as Loss
insights.statement: Only 38% of fraud reports include a dollar loss. 62% of reports either involve no financial loss or the consumer did not quantify it, suggesting the $12.5B figure significantly understates true impact.

insights.title: Relentless 23-Year Growth Trajectory
insights.statement: Consumer fraud complaints have compounded at 13.9% annually since 2001, rising from 326K to 6.5M. The 2024 total represents a 16.6% year-over-year increase from 2023.

insights.title: Fraud Is Deeply Concentrated
insights.statement: 3 categories hold 58% of reports. 3 payment methods hold 72% of losses. 10 metro areas hold 38% of metro volume. Fraud is not evenly distributed — it clusters sharply.
